name: Zeal
description_short: Zeal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution offering checking, savings, loans, credit cards, and investments with a 71-year operating history.
description_long:
Zeal Credit Union has been serving its membership for 71 years, as evidenced by their celebration of this milestone in their 2025 Annual Report. As a federally-insured credit union (NCUA-insured, member-owned cooperative), Zeal operates on a not-for-profit basis, meaning earnings are returned to members rather than shareholders. The organization maintains both personal and business banking divisions with multiple branch locations and ATM networks.

Zeal offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal and business checking/savings accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, money market accounts, youth savings accounts, and a full spectrum of loan products. Their loan offerings include mortgages, home equity loans, auto loans (currently at 4.74% APR), personal loans, fresh start loans, student loans, recreational vehicle/boat financing, and business loans. They also issue Visa credit cards with rewards programs (Rewardz), provide investment services, and offer skip-a-pay and automatic payment features.

Zeal distinguishes itself through specialized lending products like recreational vehicle and boat financing with "preapproved for show season" options, fresh start loans (suggesting credit-challenged borrower inclusion), and partnership with GreenPath Financial Wellness for member financial education. Their member benefits include discounts and rewards programs, insurance programs, branch services, and scholarship offerings. Online services span mobile banking, bill pay, external transfers, and mobile wallet functionality.

As a credit union, Zeal's primary advantage is member-ownership and not-for-profit status, potentially offering more favorable rates and lower fees than for-profit banks. However, membership eligibility varies by location and employer affiliation, and their rate competitiveness depends on membership criteria. The website lacks transparency on membership requirements, specific fee structures, and detailed rate comparisons, making it difficult to assess their true value proposition against competitors.
services: Personal checking accounts with debit cards and direct deposit
Savings accounts, money market accounts, and youth savings accounts
Certificates of deposit (CDs) and individual retirement accounts (IRAs)
Auto loans at rates as low as 4.74% APR
Home equity loans at rates as low as 5.50% APR
RV, boat, and recreational vehicle loans with show-season preapproval
Personal loans and fresh start loans
Visa credit cards with contactless transactions, rewards (Rewardz), and gift card options
Business checking, savings, and business loans (secured, commercial real estate, equipment financing)
Online banking, mobile app, bill pay, skip-a-pay, and external fund transfers
pros: 71-year operating history demonstrates long-term stability and member trust
Not-for-profit structure returns earnings to members rather than external shareholders
Comprehensive loan portfolio including specialized RV/boat financing with show-season preapproval
Fresh start loans available, suggesting willingness to serve credit-challenged borrowers
GreenPath Financial Wellness partnership provides free financial education and counseling
Full suite of digital services including mobile banking, bill pay, and mobile wallet
Member benefits program includes insurance, scholarships, and discounts
cons: Membership eligibility not clearly disclosed on website—potential membership restrictions based on location or employer affiliation
Auto loan rates of 4.74% APR are not particularly competitive compared to online lenders and larger credit unions
Limited rate transparency—specific rates for checking, savings, and personal loans not displayed on homepage
No information about monthly fees, overdraft fees, or other service charges
Website does not clearly explain membership requirements or how to qualify
best_for: Members seeking a full-service financial institution with local branch access and personalized service
RV and boat buyers needing specialized financing with preapproval at show events
Borrowers with damaged credit seeking fresh start loan options
Members wanting free financial wellness education and counseling resources
diagnosis: Zeal Credit Union is best for members who qualify for membership and prioritize local branch access, personalized service, and not-for-profit alignment. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is unclear on the website, and their publicly displayed rates are not exceptionally competitive, so prospective members should verify eligibility and compare rates before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Zeal — Credit Union in Livonia, MI | CreditDoc
meta_description: Zeal — NCUA-insured credit union in Livonia, MI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
